concerns.text: FR-005 states 'VIF > 5 indicates moderate definitional dependency... VIF > 10 indicates severe' but does not explicitly acknowledge that for census data, VIF measures collinearity in the finite dataset rather than population-level multicollinearity. Clarify that VIF interpretation is descriptive.

SC-011 requires ANOVA for group comparisons on census data. For complete census, ANOVA assumptions are irrelevant as group differences are exact descriptive facts. Consider replacing with 'descriptive comparison metrics (mean difference, variance ratio)' without significance testing.

SC-001 states 'high completeness across all records' but SC-006 quantifies '≥95%' for Phase 2+ invariants. For consistency, SC-001 should explicitly state the quantitative threshold (≥95%) for required invariant fields in Phase 1.
